description: Confounder: The model assumes that with lipschitz=True, the zero-initialized linear layer (d

_meta.explanation: The rule 'When lipschitz=True, AdaLayerNormContinuous initializes to near-identity mapping because rms_norm_no_affine of zero-initialized scale/shift yields near-zero values, making (1 + scale) ≈ 1 and shift ≈ 0.' does NOT hold here. The model assumes that with lipschitz=True, the zero-initialized linear layer (due to emb=0) yields near-identity behavior, but here emb is all ones, so the linear layer output isn't zero. However, the model might still expect normalization to suppress the scale/shift—yet rms_norm_no_affine preserves direction and unit norm, so scale ≈ 1/√d per element, making (1 + scale) significantly deviate from 1.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'When lipschitz=True, AdaLayerNormContinuous initializes to near-identity mapping because rms_norm_no_affine of zero-initialized scale/shift yields near-zero values, making (1 + scale) ≈ 1 and shift ≈ 0.' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
